title: Surge Arrester Specification Extractor
tagline: AI-powered parameter extraction from surge arrester PDF specifications — upload, extract, download filled Excel in minutes.
description: JVS Surge Arrester Specification Extractor is an internal AI tool that automatically extracts 36 technical parameters from surge arrester specification PDF documents. Engineers upload one or more PDFs through a browser-based interface, and the system uses a locally hosted 32-billion parameter language model (qwen2.5:32b via Ollama) to identify and extract parameters such as rated voltage, MCOV, TOV capability, creepage distance, residual discharge levels, and mechanical properties. The output is a structured Excel report with extracted values, units, confidence levels (High/Medium/Low), and source page numbers — ready for engineer validation. Supports both digital and scanned PDFs. Runs entirely on the company LAN with no cloud dependency.

screenshots.caption: UI showing the upload and extraction process
Extraction complete — 36 parameters processed with High/Medium/Low confidence summary and one-click Excel download

highlights: Extracts 36 technical parameters from surge arrester PDF specifications automatically
Supports both digital PDFs (text extraction) and scanned PDFs (Tesseract OCR at 300 DPI)
Confidence scoring per parameter — High / Medium / Low with source page number
32B parameter local LLM (qwen2.5:32b) — no API cost, no data leaves the network
Background threading — browser stays responsive during 30-40 minute extraction
V0 system prompt with engineer validation workflow built in for continuous improvement

items: Parameter definitions and system prompt content (V0 — under engineer review)
Customer specification PDFs and extracted data
Server IP addresses and internal network configuration
Flask service configuration and NSSM deployment details
